In the demo, the speaker argues that many internal AI tools do not need the largest possible model.

They say a smaller seven-billion-parameter model can often run on a single GPU, respond faster, and cost less to operate. The speaker presents this as a practical deployment tradeoff rather than a universal rule. They also say teams should benchmark their own tasks, because larger models still perform better on some broad reasoning workloads.

The speaker does not claim that smaller labs will overtake the biggest companies. They frame the point more narrowly: smaller models can be enough for targeted workflows when latency and hardware budgets matter.
